title: Check if a nested chain of objects is valid
snippet: A core variable type in BrightScript is the associative array. These arrays allow objects to be accessed usingkeys. The access methods I’ve seen used most often are dots or brackets.Here’s an example using dots to access a deeply nested associative array.movie = { data: { overview: { ...

title: A better way to check if something is valid in BrightScript
snippet: If there is one thing I’ve learned from working in code for so long it’s that negative conditions in if statementscan easily trip people up.
